title: polypodium dryopteris var. robertianum
intro: Polypodium dryopteris var. robertianum, also known as Western Polypody, is a type of fern plant that belongs to the Polypodiaceae family. It is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Western Polypody include reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to act as a diuretic and aid in digestion. Western Polypody can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in topical applications, such as salves and creams, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
